content.problemStatement:
Given a `N x N` square matrix, find the sum of both primary as well as secondary diagonal elements.

For eg. in the following matrix:



Sum of primary and secondary diagonal element = 3 + 2 + 0 + 4 + 5 = 14


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer NNN, denoting the no. of rows and columns in input matrix
Next NNN lines contain NNN space separated integers, the elements of the matrix.


Output Format

Output on a single line, the sum of diagonals elements.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with an integer N denoting the size of the square matrix, followed by N lines each containing N space‑separated non‑negative integers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the sum of the primary and secondary diagonal elements of the given matrix. The centre element of an odd‑sized matrix is counted only once.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Primary diagonal elements are 3, 2, 0. Secondary diagonal elements are 5, 2, 4. The centre element 2 appears in both diagonals but is counted once, giving 3+2+0+5+4 = 14.
Primary diagonal: 1 + 4 = 5. Secondary diagonal: 2 + 3 = 5. No overlap, total = 10.
A 1×1 matrix has only one element which belongs to both diagonals, counted once.

solution.approach: For each test case we iterate over the matrix once. While reading element a[i][j] we add it to the answer if it lies on the primary diagonal (i==j) or on the secondary diagonal (j==N-1-i). When N is odd the centre element satisfies both conditions, but we add it only once by using an else‑if for the secondary check. This runs in O(N^2) time per test case and uses O(1) extra space.
